# Figure description: скв_391.jpg

## Figure Type

This is a **composite well log / borehole lithological-stratigraphic column** (комплексный геологический разрез скважины or литолого-стратиграфическая колонка), typical of Soviet-era deep exploration drilling documentation. It appears to be a multi-track well log presented on a continuous vertical strip, combining lithological description columns with one or more geophysical curve tracks (likely gamma-ray, resistivity, or spontaneous potential logs) plotted against depth. The format is consistent with standard Soviet бурение (drilling) report documentation used in regional geological surveys of this period.

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **single deep borehole or possibly a composite section** from the northern Kazakhstan survey area described in Report 36572. The log extends over a considerable depth range — based on the length of the strip and Soviet standard scales, likely spanning **several hundred to potentially 1,000–2,000+ metres** of section. The document appears to run continuously from near-surface deposits down through multiple stratigraphic units. Given the regional context (map sheets M-42 and N-42, 1:200,000), this likely represents one of the key parametric or structural boreholes drilled as part of the geophysical-geological integration program. Specific borehole numbers (e.g., скв-1, скв-2, or similar) and geographic/sheet references would appear in the header, though they are not fully legible at this resolution.

## Key Content

The figure contains several distinct vertical tracks running the full length of the log:

**Left side (columns 1–3):** Appear to contain:
- Stratigraphic/age assignments (системы, отделы, ярусы — systems, series, stages) in narrow text columns
- Possibly formation/свита (suite) names
- Depth markers at regular intervals

**Central left track:** A **lithological column** showing rock type symbols (hatching, dots, dashes) representing alternating lithologies — likely interbedded sandstones, siltstones, clays/argillites, and possibly carbonates or volcanics at depth. The column shows considerable variability suggesting a sedimentary-volcanic or mixed sequence.

**Central geophysical tracks (2 prominent wiggle curves):** Two clearly visible **geophysical log curves** with significant amplitude variation:
- The **left wiggle curve** shows high-frequency, moderate-amplitude oscillations throughout — consistent with a **gamma-ray (ГК)** or **spontaneous potential (ПС)** log
- The **right/central wiggle curve** shows broader, higher-amplitude variations — possibly **resistivity (КС or БК)** or **induced polarization** response
- Both curves show notably **high amplitude anomalies** in the upper-middle portion of the section, suggesting lithological contrasts or potentially mineralised intervals
- The curves become more subdued and regular in certain depth intervals, suggesting more homogeneous (possibly crystalline basement or thick clay) sequences

**Right side (multiple narrow columns):** Appear to contain:
- Sample/core recovery data
- Drilling parameters
- Mineralogical or geochemical assay results (possibly metal content annotations)
- Cross-reference notes to laboratory analyses

**Notable features:**
- Several **distinct breaks or boundaries** in the log pattern are visible, likely representing major unconformities or formation contacts
- There appear to be **annotation clusters** at certain depth intervals on the right side, possibly indicating anomalous geochemical values or mineralisation indicators
- The section appears to pass through **at least 3–5 major stratigraphic packages** based on changes in log character

## Text and Labels

Due to the low resolution and faded nature of this Soviet-era document, full transcription is not possible, but the following elements are discernible:

**Header (top of document):** Contains a multi-line title block typical of Soviet drilling logs, likely reading something along the lines of:
- Организация/Предприятие (Organization/Enterprise) — likely a Soviet geological survey entity
- Title text possibly: **«ЛИТОЛОГО-СТРАТИГРАФИЧЕСКАЯ КОЛОНКА СКВАЖИНЫ №___»** or **«КОМПЛЕКСНЫЙ ГЕОЛОГИЧЕСКИЙ РАЗРЕЗ»**
- Sheet/area references: possibly **М-42** or **N-42** series

**Column headers (top of each track):** Standard Soviet log headers would include:
- **«Глубина, м»** (Depth, m)
- **«Стратиграфия»** (Stratigraphy)
- **«Литология»** (Lithology)
- **«Керн»** (Core) or **«Выход керна, %»** (Core recovery, %)
- Geophysical curve identifiers such as **«ПС»**, **«КС»**, **«ГК»**, **«НГК»**

**Depth scale:** Gradations visible along the left margin, appearing to be in **metres**, with major divisions likely every 50 or 100 m

**Footer/legend block (bottom right):** A small box visible at the bottom right corner — typical Soviet log convention for scale bar, authorship, date, and approval signatures. Text likely includes date references consistent with **1983–1984** fieldwork period.

**Marginal annotations:** Scattered text annotations along the right margin at various depths, possibly formation names (свиты), age designations (e.g., **С₁**, **D₃**, **О**, **Є** for Carboniferous, Devonian, Ordovician, Cambrian), or mineralogical notes.

## Research Value

This borehole log provides **direct subsurface lithological and petrophysical constraints** on the stratigraphic section penetrated at one of the key drilling sites within the M-42/N-42 survey area, allowing correlation between surface geophysical anomalies (seismic, gravity, magnetic) and actual rock types and formation boundaries at depth. The geophysical curve variations — particularly any high-resistivity or anomalous gamma-ray intervals — can be used to identify potentially **mineralised horizons, ore-controlling structures, or basement configuration**, which is central to evaluating the area's mineral potential. For an AI research assistant, this log answers the fundamental question: *"What rocks are actually present at depth, and do their geophysical signatures match the surface anomalies that motivated the drilling?"* — making it a key ground-truth document for the entire regional survey.
